petitioner_background: The petitioner sought classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability. No details about the petitioner's field of work, nationality, or career qualifications are provided in the decision text. The petition was filed at the Texas Service Center.
outcome_reasoning: The AAO dismissed the appeal as moot because USCIS records indicated that the petitioner was granted lawful permanent residence on May 4, 2013. Because the beneficiary already obtained LPR status, further pursuit of the matter was unnecessary. The AAO did not reach the merits of whether the petitioner established extraordinary ability through extensive documentation and sustained national or international acclaim.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: The appeal was dismissed as moot because the petitioner had already been granted lawful permanent residence on May 4, 2013, rendering further adjudication of the extraordinary ability petition unnecessary.
